title: Thank you & sorry
pedagogy: Vocabulary: ขอบคุณ (high+dead→low, low+live→mid), ขอโทษ (high+live→rising, low+dead-long→falling), ไม่เป็นไร (low+mai ek→falling, mid+live→mid, mid... ไร: low+live(ไ◌)→mid). Minimal pair ไม่/ไม้/ใหม่/ไหม covers mai ek and mai tho on low class vs ห-nam high class.

exercises.title: Thanks, sorry — and letting it go
body: Three phrases carry most Thai social grace: thank you, sorry, and “it's nothing”.
ไม่เป็นไร answers both a thank-you and an apology — and it's a worldview: don't let small things ruffle you.
Someone bumps you, the food is late, it rains on your plans: ไม่เป็นไร.

exercises.title: ไม่ is falling — and that matters
body: The ไม่ in ไม่เป็นไร is falling: low-class ม + mai ek.
Change the tone and you change the word: mái is wood, mài is new, mǎi is silk (or a question).
Thais famously chain them: ไม้ใหม่ไม่ไหม้ — “new wood doesn't burn”. Time to tell them apart by ear.

exercises.context: Rush hour on the BTS. The train lurches and you step hard on someone's foot. You're a man.
exercises.question: What do you say?

choices.thai: ขอบคุณครับ

exercises.explanation: You apologize: ขอโทษครับ. ไม่เป็นไร is what the OTHER person says to forgive you — saying it yourself would wave off your own apology.
